coverage_analysis_memo.body:
COVERAGE ANALYSIS MEMORANDUM

File / Exposure:   EXP-2023-04471-REYES-BI
Claim:             CLM-2023-04471
Insured:           Northwind Logistics, LLC
Policy:            NWL-CA-024471 (CA 00 01 ISO Commercial Auto)
Coverage Part:     CP-NWL-2023-BI — Auto Bodily Injury Liability
Loss Date:         03/13/2023
Claimant:          Janet Reyes (PTY-CLM-REYES)
Prepared:          2025-01-30

─────────────────────────────────────────────
I. COVERAGE QUESTIONS AND FINDINGS
─────────────────────────────────────────────

1. POLICY IN FORCE ON LOSS DATE — CONFIRMED (probability: 1.0)

The declarations page states the policy period as '01/01/2023 12:01 A.M. to 01/01/2024 12:01 A.M. (Standard Time)' (DOC-001). The crash report and dispatch log confirm the loss occurred on 03/13/2023 at 16:42 hrs (DOC-002; DOC-004). The loss date falls squarely within the policy period.

2. COVERED AUTO — CONFIRMED (probability: 1.0)

Unit 14 (2019 Freightliner M2-106, VIN 1FVACWDT9KHKL4218) is listed in the Schedule of Covered Autos on the declarations page with Liability, Comp, and Collision coverage (DOC-001). Covered Autos Symbol 7 ('Specifically Described Autos') applies to liability (DOC-001). The police report independently identifies V-1 as the same unit and cross-references policy NWL-CA-024471 (DOC-002).

3. AUTHORIZED DRIVER / PERMISSIVE USE / ON-DUTY OPERATION — CONFIRMED (probability: 0.97)

The dispatch log records Aliyah as the assigned driver of Unit 14 for route BR-Mon-A on 03/13/2023 with 'No reassignment, no swap.' It further states: 'Vehicle use during the return leg (Adamo → Causeway terminal) is on-duty per Northwind operations manual §4.3 (truck must be returned to terminal before driver clock-out).' (DOC-004). Aliyah's recorded statement corroborates: 'I was returning to the terminal. That's, uh, that's the protocol. Log the truck in, do the post-trip inspection, drop the keys.' (DOC-003). The incident occurred at 16:42, with scheduled check-out at 17:00 and ETA to terminal of 17:00 (DOC-004). The operations manual itself (§4.3) has not been produced; a residual 3% uncertainty is noted.

4. DRIVER ELIGIBILITY — SUBSTANTIALLY CONFIRMED, ONE OPEN ITEM (probability: 0.97)

The declarations page requires a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14 (DOC-001). The dispatch log records CDL #A2241-FL, expiring 2024-08-31 — valid on the loss date (DOC-004). The police report independently notes 'FL CDL A2241' (DOC-002). However, the 'A2241' prefix may suggest a Class A CDL. Under FMCSA regulations, a Class A CDL authorizes operation of Class B vehicles; if confirmed, this satisfies the Class B requirement. The full MVR has not been produced to the file. This open item carries low but non-zero significance.

5. BODILY INJURY TO CLAIMANT — CONFIRMED (probability: 0.95)

The police report codes Reyes as 'Possible Injury (C)' and documents scene transport to Tampa General for neck and lower-back pain (DOC-002). This satisfies the threshold for a covered BI claim. Medical records are outstanding and will bear on damages valuation, not on the coverage determination.

6. APPLICABLE EXCLUSIONS — NONE IDENTIFIED (probability: 0.94 that no exclusion applies)

The endorsement schedule on the declarations page lists four endorsements, none of which exclude BI coverage for a third-party rear-end collision (DOC-001). No alcohol, drug, or intentional conduct issues are noted (DOC-002). The UM rejection is a first-party coverage issue and is not relevant to this third-party BI exposure. The full ISO CA 00 01 policy form has not been reproduced in the file; a 6% residual is retained for unproduced policy language or endorsements.

7. APPLICABLE LIMIT AND DEDUCTIBLE

BI Liability: $1,000,000 Combined Single Limit per accident, $0 deductible (DOC-001). No per-person sublimit is listed. The claimant is a single BI claimant; the full $1,000,000 CSL is potentially available.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
II. COVERAGE OUTCOME DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────

  Clean Coverage:        88%
  Reservation of Rights: 10%
  Denial:                 2%

The weight of evidence supports clean coverage. The ROR probability is driven by two open items: (a) the CDL class discrepancy and absence of the produced MVR; (b) the unproduced operations manual §4.3. Neither is expected to be dispositive on the present record.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
III. OPEN ITEMS / EVIDENCE THAT WOULD SHIFT THE DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Driver Aliyah's current MVR — to confirm CDL class (A vs. B) and validity as of 03/13/2023.
2. Full ISO CA 00 01 policy form and any endorsements not listed on the declarations page.
3. Northwind operations manual §4.3 — to independently confirm that the terminal return leg is on-duty use.
4. Medical records from Tampa General and treating providers — relevant to BI damages valuation.
5. Any driver exclusion endorsement specifically naming or excluding Aliyah (would materially shift distribution toward denial if present).

─────────────────────────────────────────────
IV. COVERAGE LIMIT SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────

  Coverage Part:           CP-NWL-2023-BI
  Form:                    CA 00 01 (ISO Commercial Auto)
  Limit:                   $1,000,000 CSL per accident
  Deductible:              $0
  SIR:                     None
  UM/UIM (first party):    Rejected (not applicable to this exposure)

─────────────────────────────────────────────
NOTE: This memorandum presents a quantified analysis of the coverage record. It does not constitute a coverage position, a claim payment direction, or legal advice. The adjuster retains authority to select the coverage path.

ror_letter.body:
RESERVATION OF RIGHTS LETTER — DRAFT

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Insured:        Northwind Logistics, LLC
      Policy No.:     NWL-CA-024471
      Claim No.:      CLM-2023-04471
      Date of Loss:   March 13, 2023
      Claimant:       Janet Reyes
      Coverage:       Commercial Auto Bodily Injury Liability

Dear Northwind Logistics Representative:

This letter is sent on behalf of [CARRIER NAME] in connection with the above-referenced claim arising from the March 13, 2023 traffic collision on Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, Florida.

We have accepted this claim for investigation under a reservation of rights. This means that we are investigating and, as appropriate, defending this matter, but we reserve all rights under the policy, at law, and in equity, including the right to disclaim or limit coverage based on any policy provision, condition, or exclusion that may apply.

The basis for this reservation is set forth below.

I. APPLICABLE POLICY PROVISIONS

Policy Number NWL-CA-024471 is a Commercial Automobile Policy, Form CA 00 01 (ISO Commercial Auto), with a policy period of January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2024. The declarations page states:

  'COVERED AUTOS SYMBOLS: Liability (Sym. 7) ... Specifically Described Autos'
  (DOC-001, Declarations Page)

  'DRIVER ELIGIBILITY: All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience.'
  (DOC-001, Declarations Page)

II. OPEN COVERAGE QUESTIONS

A. DRIVER CDL CLASS

The declarations page requires that the driver of Unit 14 hold a valid CDL Class B. The dispatch log for March 13, 2023 records driver Marcus Aliyah's CDL as '#A2241-FL, exp. 2024-08-31' (DOC-004). The police report similarly records 'FL CDL A2241' (DOC-002). The 'A' designation may indicate a Class A CDL. While a Class A CDL generally authorizes operation of Class B vehicles under federal licensing rules, we have not yet received a certified copy of Mr. Aliyah's Motor Vehicle Record to confirm the CDL class and its validity as of the date of loss. We are requesting that MVR now and reserve the right to revisit coverage if the documentation reveals any CDL eligibility issue.

B. FULL POLICY FORM AND ENDORSEMENTS

This analysis is based on the declarations page produced to the file (DOC-001). The complete ISO CA 00 01 policy form and all endorsements have not been independently reproduced in our file. We reserve the right to assert any exclusion, condition, or limitation contained in the full policy documents.

III. CURRENT INVESTIGATION STATUS

We are actively investigating this matter, including obtaining:
  1. A certified copy of driver Aliyah's current MVR;
  2. The complete CA 00 01 policy form and all endorsements;
  3. Medical records from Tampa General Hospital and any treating providers;
  4. The Northwind Logistics operations manual (§4.3).

IV. RIGHTS RESERVED

By undertaking this investigation and any associated defense activities, [CARRIER NAME] does not waive, and expressly reserves, all rights and defenses under the policy, at law, and in equity. This reservation includes but is not limited to the right to deny coverage in whole or in part if the investigation reveals a basis for denial.

This letter is not a denial of coverage. It is an advisement that coverage may be limited or unavailable depending on the outcome of our continued investigation.

We encourage you to retain independent legal counsel if you have questions about this reservation.

Please direct inquiries to:

[ADJUSTER NAME]
[TPA / CARRIER]
[ADDRESS]
[PHONE / EMAIL]

Sincerely,

[ADJUSTER NAME], Senior Claims Specialist
On behalf of [CARRIER NAME]

[DISCLAIMER: This is a draft prepared for adjuster review. It does not constitute the carrier's coverage position and has not been reviewed by coverage counsel. The adjuster and/or coverage counsel should review this draft before issuance.]

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Insured:        Northwind Logistics, LLC
      Policy No.:     NWL-CA-024471
      Claim No.:      CLM-2023-04471
      Date of Loss:   March 13, 2023
      Claimant:       Janet Reyes
      Coverage:       Commercial Auto Bodily Injury Liability

Dear Northwind Logistics Representative:

This letter is sent on behalf of [CARRIER NAME]. We have completed an initial coverage review of the above-referenced claim.

NOTE TO ADJUSTER: This denial draft is prepared as an audit artifact representing the low-probability (2%) denial path. On the present record, no denial basis has been confirmed. This draft would require significant supplementation — specifically the identification of a confirmed coverage-defeating condition (e.g., a driver exclusion endorsement, a confirmed CDL ineligibility, or a confirmed policy exclusion) — before issuance. Do not issue this letter based on the current file.

I. POLICY PROVISIONS AT ISSUE

The policy at issue is Commercial Automobile Policy No. NWL-CA-024471, Form CA 00 01, with a policy period of January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2024 (DOC-001).

The declarations page states:

  'COVERED AUTOS SYMBOLS: Liability (Sym. 7) ............. Specifically Described Autos'
  (DOC-001)

  'DRIVER ELIGIBILITY: All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience.'
  (DOC-001)

II. BASIS FOR DENIAL [PLACEHOLDER — TO BE COMPLETED ONLY IF A CONFIRMED DENIAL BASIS IS IDENTIFIED]

[ADJUSTER/COVERAGE COUNSEL TO INSERT: The specific policy provision, exclusion, or condition that was found to apply, together with the factual basis for its application. For example: (a) a driver exclusion endorsement specifically excluding the driver; (b) confirmed CDL ineligibility; (c) a specific policy exclusion found in the full form not visible on the declarations page.]

III. RIGHTS RESERVED

[CARRIER NAME] reserves all rights and defenses under the policy, at law, and in equity.

IV. RIGHT TO INDEPENDENT COUNSEL

You have the right to retain independent legal counsel in connection with this matter.

Sincerely,

[ADJUSTER NAME], Senior Claims Specialist
On behalf of [CARRIER NAME]

[DISCLAIMER: This is a draft prepared for adjuster review only. On the current file record, the denial path carries a 2% probability. This draft requires identification of a confirmed denial basis and review by coverage counsel before issuance. Do not issue without completing Section II.]
